sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Minimal navigation header for a Mover at the decision stage — strip nav links to eliminate distraction, keep logo and a single 'Upgrade Now' CTA. The persistent nav CTA is the highest-converting element for decisive users who already know the product from their trial.

sections.name: Hero — Single Action Conversion Prompt
sections.rationale: Movers at the decision stage need one clear message and one action above the fold. HeroPrimary in its Mover variant suppresses subhead and secondary CTA, delivering a singular 'Your trial ends — upgrade now' directive. The with-social-proof variant can layer a LogoStrip beneath the CTA for silent credibility without slowing momentum.

sections.name: Silent Credibility Bar
sections.rationale: SocialProofBar in its Mover variant (logos-only, no headline) provides instant social validation without interrupting the decision flow. At the decision stage, a Mover doesn't need to read testimonials — seeing recognizable brand logos is enough to confirm they're making the right call.

sections.name: Outcome Metrics
sections.rationale: StatBlock in its Mover variant strips sources and headlines, presenting pure metric impact — e.g., '3× faster workflows,' '40% cost reduction,' '99.9% uptime.' For a Mover at the decision stage, unsourced but bold numbers reinforce the urgency and value of converting now, without requiring them to slow down and verify.

sections.name: Pricing — Upgrade Plans
sections.rationale: PricingCard is purpose-built for the decision stage. The Mover variant strips descriptions down to price and CTA only, removing friction from plan selection. Use the highlighted variant on the recommended tier so the Mover's eye is drawn to a single best choice, accelerating the upgrade decision from trial.

sections.name: Terminal Conversion Banner
sections.rationale: CTABanner in its Mover variant delivers a strong headline and single CTA with no subhead — the final push for a Mover at the decision stage who has scrolled past proof and pricing. Placed as the terminal action section, it recaptures any remaining intent before the page ends. Use the minimal variant for maximum urgency.

sections.name: Footer with Recovery CTA
sections.rationale: FooterMinimal with its with-cta variant is essential on a decision-stage conversion page targeting Movers. It recaptures departing trial users who scrolled to the bottom without converting, offering one last low-friction upgrade prompt alongside minimal legal and brand elements.

page.reasoning: minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only. Mover archetype at decision stage requires maximum focus on conversion action.
Mover variant suppresses subhead and cta_secondary — one message, one action. Decision-stage Movers need clarity without distraction.

slots.headline: Your trial ends. Your work doesn't stop.

page.reasoning: Mover variant is logos-only, no headline — silent credibility. logos-only is the highest-frequency placement for conversion-focused contexts.
Mover variant suppresses sources and headline; pure metric impact. 3-stat is the preferred default for high-impact metric clusters without attribution overhead.
Mover variant strips description; price and CTA only. Decision-stage trial users need direct pricing clarity to convert to paid.
Mover variant: strong headline, single CTA, no subhead. Centered placement provides maximum focus for terminal conversion action at decision stage.
with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages — use on pricing and signup flows. Critical for converting trial users at decision stage.
